import pandas as pd
import json
# Read full results from files
with open(var_call_kmRIS19tRbLjbUjv7A8Phvxr, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    docs = json.load(f)
with open(var_call_NtTvOxLuB398M5hJv6km8pV3, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    funding = json.load(f)

# Step 1: Identify projects started in Spring 2022 (March, April, May 2022)
from dateutil.parser import parse as dt_parse
import re
project_start_names = set()
# Regexes to find projects and their start times
project_sections = []
for doc in docs:
    # Look for lines indicating potential project entries
    lines = doc['text'].split('\n')
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if re.search(r'Project Schedule|Estimated Schedule|Updates|Description', line, re.I):
            # Get up to next 10 lines as possible schedule info
            desc = '\n'.join(lines[i:i+10])
            # Try to extract date mention
            found = re.findall(r'(Begin Construction|Advertise|Start|Complete Design|Final Design|Project start)[^a-zA-Z0-9]*[:\-]?\s*([A-Za-z]+)\s*(\d{4})', desc, re.I)
            for item in found:
                event, month, year = item
                # Only 'Begin Construction', 'Start', or similar events
                # Spring months: March, April, May
                if year == '2022' and month.strip().lower() in ['march', 'april', 'may']:
                    # Now, attempt to get project name: search backward for lines with 'Project' or plausible titles (usually previous non-empty line)
                    name = None
                    for j in range(i-1, max(i-10, 0), -1):
                        candidate = lines[j].strip()
                        if candidate and re.search(r'(Project|Improvements|Repair|Study|Facility|Slope|Park|Road|Drainage|Retaining Wall|Walkway|Structure)', candidate, re.I):
                            name = candidate
                            break
                    if not name:
                        # fallback: find any line above with non-empty string
                        name = next((lines[j].strip() for j in range(i-1, max(i-10,0), -1) if lines[j].strip()), None)
                    if name:
                        project_start_names.add(name)

# Step 2: Try to normalize project names and funding data (partial match)
funding_df = pd.DataFrame(funding)
started_projects = []
total_funding = 0
for proj_name in project_start_names:
    # Use substring match for funding table
    matched = funding_df[funding_df['Project_Name'].str.contains(proj_name[:15], case=False, na=False)]
    if len(matched):
        amt = matched['Amount'].astype(int).sum()
        total_funding += amt
        started_projects.append({'Project_Name': proj_name, 'Funding': int(amt)})
result = {'Spring_2022_Project_Count': len(started_projects), 'Total_Funding': total_funding, 'Projects': started_projects}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

import pandas as pd
import json
# Read full results from files
with open(var_call_kmRIS19tRbLjbUjv7A8Phvxr, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    docs = json.load(f)
with open(var_call_NtTvOxLuB398M5hJv6km8pV3, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    funding = json.load(f)

# Step 1: Identify projects started in Spring 2022 (March, April, May 2022)
import re
project_start_names = set()
for doc in docs:
    lines = doc['text'].split('\n')
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if re.search(r'Project Schedule|Estimated Schedule|Updates|Description', line, re.I):
            desc = '\n'.join(lines[i:i+10])
            found = re.findall(r'(Begin Construction|Advertise|Start|Complete Design|Final Design|Project start)[^a-zA-Z0-9]*[:\-]?\s*([A-Za-z]+)\s*(\d{4})', desc, re.I)
            for item in found:
                event, month, year = item
                if year == '2022' and month.strip().lower() in ['march', 'april', 'may']:
                    name = None
                    for j in range(i-1, max(i-10, 0), -1):
                        candidate = lines[j].strip()
                        if candidate and re.search(r'(Project|Improvements|Repair|Study|Facility|Slope|Park|Road|Drainage|Retaining Wall|Walkway|Structure)', candidate, re.I):
                            name = candidate
                            break
                    if not name:
                        name = next((lines[j].strip() for j in range(i-1, max(i-10,0), -1) if lines[j].strip()), None)
                    if name:
                        project_start_names.add(name)

funding_df = pd.DataFrame(funding)
started_projects = []
total_funding = 0
for proj_name in project_start_names:
    matched = funding_df[funding_df['Project_Name'].str.contains(proj_name[:15], case=False, na=False)]
    if len(matched):
        amt = matched['Amount'].astype(int).sum()
        total_funding += amt
        started_projects.append({'Project_Name': proj_name, 'Funding': int(amt)})
result = {'Spring_2022_Project_Count': len(started_projects), 'Total_Funding': total_funding, 'Projects': started_projects}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json
with open(var_call_kmRIS19tRbLjbUjv7A8Phvxr, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    docs = json.load(f)
with open(var_call_NtTvOxLuB398M5hJv6km8pV3, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    funding = json.load(f)
import pandas as pd
import re
project_start_names = set()
for doc in docs:
    # Use a simpler method: look for 'Begin Construction: March/April/May 2022'
    found = re.findall(r'([A-Za-z0-9 ,&/\'\-.]+)\n\s*(Begin Construction|Advertise|Start|Complete Design|Final Design)[^a-zA-Z0-9]*[:\-]?\s*(March|April|May)\s*2022', doc['text'], re.I)
    for item in found:
        proj_name = item[0].strip()
        project_start_names.add(proj_name)
funding_df = pd.DataFrame(funding)
started_projects = []
total_funding = 0
for proj_name in project_start_names:
    matched = funding_df[funding_df['Project_Name'].str.contains(proj_name[:12], case=False, na=False)]
    if len(matched):
        amt = matched['Amount'].astype(int).sum()
        total_funding += amt
        started_projects.append({'Project_Name': proj_name, 'Funding': int(amt)})
result = {'Spring_2022_Project_Count': len(started_projects), 'Total_Funding': total_funding, 'Projects': started_projects}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

import json
import pandas as pd
import re
with open(var_call_kmRIS19tRbLjbUjv7A8Phvxr, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    docs = json.load(f)
with open(var_call_NtTvOxLuB398M5hJv6km8pV3, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    funding = json.load(f)
project_start_names = set()
for doc in docs:
    found = re.findall(r'([A-Za-z0-9 ,&/\'".\-]+)\n\s*(Begin Construction|Advertise|Start|Complete Design|Final Design)[^a-zA-Z0-9]*[:\-]?\s*(March|April|May)\s*2022', doc['text'], re.I)
    for item in found:
        proj_name = item[0].strip()
        project_start_names.add(proj_name)
funding_df = pd.DataFrame(funding)
started_projects = []
total_funding = 0
for proj_name in project_start_names:
    matched = funding_df[funding_df['Project_Name'].str.contains(proj_name[:12], case=False, na=False)]
    if len(matched):
        amt = matched['Amount'].astype(int).sum()
        total_funding += amt
        started_projects.append({'Project_Name': proj_name, 'Funding': int(amt)})
result = {'Spring_2022_Project_Count': len(started_projects), 'Total_Funding': total_funding, 'Projects': started_projects}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))
